global.title: Key takeaways from Chile’s Re+ Cono sur
global.summary: Chile’s Energy Minister Ximena Rincón called for a more proactive approach to renewables, storage, and electrification at the RE+ Southern Cone Summit last week, highlighting the need to reduce fossil fuel reliance and invest in enabling infrastructure.

global.title: BBDF 2026: How financing standalone vs. co-located projects really works
global.summary: The “lab” session at Battery Business & Development Forum 2026 made transparent how different stakeholders evaluate and view energy storage projects between standalone and co-located options, and where views diverged.

global.title: Germany’s heat pump public discourse is positive but volatile
global.summary: Research from Germany analyzed sentiment in online news coverage to explore how heat pumps are portrayed in German public discourse. It found that while public perception is generally positive, it is neither uniform nor stable, and dipped significantly during debates around Germany’s heating law in

global.title: Solar Fabrik launches 400 W red-brown module for heritage roofs
global.summary: Solar Fabrik has introduced a 400 W red-brown glass-glass PV module designed to meet strict aesthetic requirements for historic and protected buildings, offering a 20.02% efficiency and compatibility with traditional tiled rooftops.

global.title: BBDF: German network agency to release BESS grid fee framework soon
global.summary: Germany’s Federal Network Agency (Bundesnetzagentur) is set to present a framework for future network fees in May 2026, with industry discussions highlighting how these charges could interact with flexible connection agreements and affect battery energy storage system (BESS) returns.

global.title: Can sodium-ion storage drive energy flexibility in France?
global.summary: France’s Association Environnement Juste has proposed a storage mandate for renewables projects exceeding 10 kWp in order to integrate flexibility at the source. The association argues European sodium-ion batteries could present an economically and environmentally viable solution, capable of stabili

global.title: Solbian launches solar kit for boat davits
global.summary: Available in 80 W and 108 W versions using high-efficiency SunPower Maxeon cells, the kit offers wind resistance up to 25 m/s and easy installation on unused boat surfaces, according to the manufacturer.

global.title: Sassmann develops ‘solar panel grid’ for pigeon protection
global.summary: Sassmann, a Germany-based company, has developed a modular “solar panel grid” system designed to prevent pigeons from nesting beneath photovoltaic modules.

global.title: Abu Dhabi extends solar self-supply policy to residents
global.summary: The UAE capital is expanding its solar self-supply policy to the residential sector, after first launching for the agricultural sector in February.

global.title: SolarEdge outlines path to 800 V (DC) data center infrastructure
global.summary: SolarEdge has outlined a transition pathway to integrated 800 V (DC) infrastructure for data centers, arguing that legacy alternating current (AC) systems introduce inefficiencies that could constrain the growth of AI workloads. It says rising compute demand and next-gen NVIDIA GPUs are increasing u

global.title: Middle East Energy Crisis: Comparing Operating Costs for Diesel vs Electric Trucks

global.title: NIO Sales Grow 136% Year Over Year
global.summary: While some other EV automakers have had a hard — or very hard — start to the year, NIO was absolutely popping in March. global.title: XPENG Sales Down 17% Year Over Year
global.summary: XPENG’s 2026 vehicle deliveries continue to be notably lower than a year before. Though, this month, they at least grew significantly compared to the month before. With 27,415 deliveries in March, XPENG’s vehicle sales were down 17.44% compared to March 2025 (33,205 deliveries), but they
